steps: Take a double overhand grip that's about shoulder width. Flex your elbows while keeping your elbows tucked in. Try not to let them flare out.
Curl until your forearm presses into your bicep. Then fully extend your elbows at the bottom of each rep.

name: Barbell Overhead Tricep Extension
steps: Be careful getting the bar into place overhead. Push your elbows forward while keeping your elbows tucked in.
Look for what feels like a stretch in your triceps. Extend your elbows until your fists are pointed at the ceiling.

name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
steps: Take a shoulder width, double overhand or mixed grip.
Push your hips back while leaving your knees mostly extended. Look for a stretch in your hamstrings.
When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward until you're back in a standing position.

name: Barbell High Bar Squat
steps: Place the bar on top of your traps.
Push your hips back and break at the knees. Push your elbows forward to keep yourself from getting pitched too far forward.
Aim to get your hamstrings to parallel with the floor.
Push your heels into the floor and squat back to a standing position.

name: Barbell Low Bar Squat
steps: Pull your shoulder blades back really hard and place the bar on your posterior delts.
Push your hips back and break at the knees to initiate the squat.
Aim to get your hamstrings to parallel with the ground. Push your heels into the floor and squat back up to a standing position.

name: Dumbbell Wrist Extension
steps: Grab two dumbbells with an overhand grip and lay your forearms across your knees.
Let your wrists flex fully, then extend your wrists.

name: Dumbbell Bulgarian Split Squat
steps: Find a box or bench that is roughly knee height or a few inches lower. (The lower the box is the lower the mobility requirement).
You can either place your toes or the top of your foot on the box. Point your front foot forward or slightly turned outwards and break at the knees and hips simultaneously.
Aim to get your front leg to hit parallel depth (hamstring parallel with the ground) or lower.
The further forward your front foot is, the longer the Range of Motion is at your hips. The further back your front foot is, the longer the ROM at your knees.

name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Row
steps: Hinge forward at the hips while maintaining a flat back. Try to get your torso as close to parallel with the ground as your mobility will allow for.
Let your arms hang in front of you. Pull your elbows back towards the ceiling while flaring your elbows outward.

name: Bodyweight Russian Twist
steps: Hover your heels just off the ground.
Stick your chest out and maintain a flat back. Make all the movement happen at your mid and upper spine.
Point your chest to the left, then rotate at the spine until your chest is pointed right.

name: Side Plank Up Down
steps: Start with your elbow and hip on the ground. Either neutral fist or flat hand on the ground.
Begin in a side plank position, and then drop your hips to the floor.
Pull your hips back to the ceiling to finish the rep.

name: Side Plank Reach Through
steps: Begin in an elbow side plank position.
Reach for the ceiling with your off hand.
Rotate at the mid and upper spine and reach through the open space between the floor and your torso.
Reach backwards, and then rotate back into the starting position.

name: Long Lever Plank
steps: Start in a hand plank position and walk your hands further out until your ears are between your biceps.
Hold in this position for the allotted amount of time.
Make sure you keep a flat back for the whole set.

name: Elbow Side Plank
steps: Press your elbow into the floor.
Pick your hips up off the ground and maintain this position for the allotted amount of time.
Make sure you don't let your chest fall forward or backward.

name: Hand Side Plank
steps: Press your hand into the ground and pick up your hip off the ground.
Open your chest and maintain this position for the allotted amount of time.

name: Hand Plank
steps: Start in a kneeling position with your hands planted on the ground.
Pick your knees up off the ground. Hold in this position with a flat back.

name: Hollow Hold
steps: Lay flat on the ground with your arms extended over head.
Pick your upper back and your legs off the ground. Your body should be in a banana shape.
Hold in this position.

name: Barbell Calf Jump
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Shift your weight onto the balls of your feet and raise stay flat footed. Using your calf muscles, mimic a jumping motion but don't leave the floor.
Explode onto your tip toes and hold for a second.
Bring your heels back to the floor then initiate the next rep.

name: Barbell Coan Deadlift
steps: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Bend at the hips and knees to grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, positioning your hands just outside of your hips.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, lift the barbell off the floor by extending your hips and knees.
As you lift the barbell, pull your shoulders back and squeeze your glutes to fully extend your hips at the top of the movement.

name: Barbell Forward Lunge
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell at your shoulders with an overhand grip.
Step forward with your right foot and bend both knees to lower your body until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Push off your right foot to return to the starting position.

name: Barbell Front Rack Step Up
steps: Stand in front of a step or bench with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell at your shoulders with an overhand grip.
Step up onto the bench with your right foot, pushing off your foot to raise your body up onto the bench.
Bring your left foot up onto the bench and stand up straight, keeping the barbell at your shoulders.
Step back down with your left foot, followed by your right foot, to return to the starting position.

name: Barbell Glute Bridge
steps: Lie on your back on the floor with your knees bent and your feet flat on the ground. Place a barbell across your hips and grasp it with both hands.
Keeping your arms straight, push through your heels to lift your hips off the ground and raise the barbell.
Hold the bridge position for a moment, then slowly lower your hips back to the starting position.

name: Barbell Heels Up Back Squat
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your heels on a plate or low step.
Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position.
Push through your heels to return to the starting position.

name: Barbell Heels Up Front Squat
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and feet on a plate. Place the bar in a front rack position.
Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position, keeping your heels raised off the ground.
Push through your heels to return to the starting position.

name: Barbell Hooklying Bench Press
steps: Start in a bench press position with your feet planted on the bench and knees bent.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, positioning your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and lower it to your chest, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.

name: Barbell Landmine Row
steps: Position a barbell in a landmine or corner of a room and stand facing it with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the end of the barbell with a neutral grip, keeping your arms straight and your shoulders relaxed.
Bend at the hips and knees to lower your torso until it is almost parallel to the ground.
Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together as you row the weight up.

name: Barbell Larsen Bench Press
steps: Start in a bench press position with your legs elevated. Try to keep your knees with a slight bend in them.
Unrack the bar then initiate the rep.
Bring the bar to your torso and tap your ribcage before pressing back to the ceiling.

name: Barbell Lateral Lunge
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell at your shoulders with an overhand grip.
Step out to the side with your right foot and bend your right knee to lower your body until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Push off your right foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite side by stepping out with your left foot.

name: Barbell Long Landmine Row
steps: Position a barbell in a landmine or corner of a room and stand facing it with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the end of the barbell with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight and your shoulders relaxed.
Bend at the hips and knees to lower your torso until it is almost parallel to the ground.
Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together as you row the weight up.

name: Barbell Meadows Row
steps: Position a barbell in a landmine or corner of a room and stand facing sideways with a staggered stance and your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the end of the barbell with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight and your shoulders relaxed.
Bend at the hips and knees to lower your torso until it is almost parallel to the ground.
Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together as you row the weight up.
*Named after the late John Meadows

name: Barbell Pause Box Squat
steps: Stand in front of a box or bench with your feet shoulder-width apart and a barbell resting on your upper back.
Lower your body into a squat position, sitting back onto the box and pausing for a moment before standing back up.
Push through your heels to return to the standing position.

name: Barbell Pause Squat
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and a barbell resting on your upper back.
Lower your body into a squat position, pausing for a moment at the bottom of the movement before standing back up.
Push through your heels to return to the standing position.

name: Barbell Pronated Pendlay Row
steps: Start hinged forward with a flat back and the plates resting on the floor. Make sure your elbows are fully extended.
Pull the bar to your sternum.
Return the plates back to the floor and let the bar come to a full dead stop.
Then initiate the next rep.

name: Barbell Pronated Row
steps: Grab the barbell with an overhand grip. Hinge forward while keeping your back flat until your torso is about parallel with the ground.
Let your arms hang freely.
Pull the bar to your torso while tucking your elbows in slightly.
Extend your elbows fully before initiating the next rep.

name: Barbell Reverse Deadlift
steps: Start by standing with your feet about hip-width apart, with your toes pointed slightly outward.
Place a barbell on the floor behind you, with the weight plates on either side.
Bend down and grab the barbell with an overhand grip, your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Keep your back straight and your shoulders down as you grip the bar.
Stand up straight, lifting the barbell off the ground and pulling it toward your hips. Keep your elbows close to your body and your shoulders down as you do this.

name: Barbell Reverse Lunge
steps: Start by standing with your feet about hip-width apart, with a barbell on your shoulders. Your feet should be slightly pointed outward, and your shoulders should be down and relaxed.
Step backward with one foot, lowering your body until your front thigh is parallel to the ground and your back knee is hovering just above the ground.
Push off your back foot, returning to the starting position.

name: Bodyweight Reverse Step Up
steps: Start with a box or bench behind you and one of your feet on the box/bench.
The balls of your feet should be planted on the box/bench.
Drive through the ball of your foot extending your knees until you're upright and standing on the box.

name: Barbell Seated Calf Raise
steps: Start by sitting on a bench with a barbell resting on your knees. Your feet should be flat on the ground, about hip-width apart.
Place your hands on the barbell for support, and lift your heels off the ground.
Hold this position for a moment, then slowly lower your heels back to the ground.

name: Barbell Shrug
steps: Start by standing with your feet about hip-width apart, with a barbell in front of you.
Bend down and grab the barbell with an overhand grip, your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Keep your back straight and your shoulders down as you grip the bar.
Stand up straight, lifting the barbell off the ground and pulling it toward your hips. Keep your elbows close to your body and your shoulders down as you do this.
Once the barbell is at your hips, raise your shoulders as high as you can, holding the position for a moment.

name: Barbell Side Step Up
steps: Start by standing with your feet about hip-width apart, with a barbell on your shoulders. Your feet should be slightly pointed outward, and your shoulders should be down and relaxed.
Place a step or box beside you, and step up onto it with one foot. Your other foot should remain on the ground.
Push off your grounded foot, raising your body up onto the step.
Step back down with the same foot, returning to the starting position.

name: Barbell Snatch Grip Deadlift
steps: Start by standing with your feet about hip-width apart, with your toes pointed slightly outward.
Place a barbell on the floor in front of you, with the weight plates on either side. Make sure that the bar is directly in line with your feet.
Bend down and grab the barbell with an overhand grip, your hands much wider than shoulder-width apart. Keep your back straight and your shoulders down as you grip the bar.
Stand up straight, lifting the barbell off the ground and pulling it toward your hips. Keep your elbows close to your body and your shoulders down as you do this.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the ground, making sure to keep your back straight and your shoulders down throughout the movement.

name: Barbell Snatch Grip Romanian Deadlift
steps: Start by standing with your feet about hip-width apart. The bar should be in a rack or box so it's lifted off the ground.
Grab the barbell with an overhand grip, your hands should be much wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hing forward at the hips keeping your knees mostly extended. Hinge until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
When you feel the stretch, push your hips forward until you are back in a standing position.

name: Barbell Split Squat
steps: Take a large step forward with one leg, and lower your body until your back knee nearly touches the ground. Your front knee should be bent at a 90-degree angle, and your back leg should be straight.
Push through the heel of your front foot to raise your body back to the starting position.

name: Barbell Spoto Press
steps: Start on the bench with your shoulder blades pulled back.
As you initiate the first rep, tuck your elbows slightly until the bar is a few inches off your chest.
Pause just off your chest for 1-3 seconds. Then press explosively.

name: Barbell Step Up Balance
steps: Begin by standing in front of a sturdy box or bench that is about knee-height, holding a barbell across your upper back and shoulders, using an overhand grip.
Place your right foot on the box and push through the heel to lift your body up and onto the box, keeping your left foot off the ground.
Step back down with your right foot, lowering your left foot to the ground.

name: Barbell Step Up
steps: Begin by standing in front of a sturdy box or bench that is about knee-height, holding a barbell across your upper back and shoulders, using an overhand grip.
Place your right foot on the box and push through the heel to lift your body up and onto the box.
Step back down with your right foot.

name: Barbell Suitcase Deadlift
steps: Begin by standing with your feet hip-width apart and holding a barbell at one side of your body, with your palms facing your thighs.
Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, bend at the hips and knees to lower the barbell down towards the ground.
As the barbell reaches the ground, use your legs and glutes to push through your heels and extend your hips and knees to raise your body back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the desired number of reps, then switch sides and repeat the exercise with the barbell on the opposite side of your body.

name: Barbell Supinated Pendlay Row
steps: Begin by standing in front of a barbell with your feet about hip-width apart and your palms facing towards your body.
Bend at the hips and knees to grab the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip, positioning the barbell about an inch away from your shins.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, use your back muscles to lift the barbell straight up towards your chest.
As the barbell reaches your chest, rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing towards your body (supinated grip), and then lower the barbell back to the starting position.

name: Barbell Supinated Row
steps: Begin by standing in front of a barbell with your feet about hip-width apart.
Bend at the hips and knees to grab the barbell with a supinated (underhand) grip, positioning the barbell about an inch away from your shins.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, use your back muscles to lift the barbell straight up towards your chest.
As the barbell reaches your chest, pause briefly, and then lower the barbell back to the starting position.

name: Barbell Tap Pause Squat
steps: Begin by standing in front of a sturdy box or bench that is about knee-height, holding a barbell across your upper back and shoulders, using an overhand grip.
Sit back and lower your body down until your glutes tap the top of the box, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Push through your heels to extend your hips and stand up, bringing your body back to the starting position.

name: Barbell Toes Up Calf Raise
steps: Begin by standing on a raised surface (such as a step or box) with the balls of your feet on the edge of the surface and your heels hanging off. Hold a barbell across your upper back and shoulders, using an overhand grip.
Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, push through the balls of your feet to raise your heels up as high as you can.
Lower your heels back down to the starting position, allowing your calves to stretch.

name: Barbell Zercher Good Morning
steps: Begin by standing with your feet about hip-width apart and holding a barbell in the crook of your elbows, with your palms facing towards your body.
Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, bend at the hips and lower your torso forward until it is nearly parallel to the ground.
Push through your heels to extend your hips and raise your body back to the starting position.

name: Barbell Zercher Squat
steps: Begin by standing with your feet about hip-width apart and holding a barbell in the crook of your arms, near your belly. Your palms should be facing towards your body.
Sit back and lower your body down until your thighs are parallel to the ground, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Push through your heels to extend your hips and stand back up to the starting position.

name: Barbell Z Press
steps: Begin by sitting on the ground with your legs extended and a barbell in your hands, with your palms facing forward and your elbows bent.
Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, lift the barbell up from your chest and press it straight up above your head.
Lower the barbell back down to your chest.

name: Assisted Bulgarian Split Squat
steps: Grab a stick of some sort or hold onto something stable (rack, bench).
Grab a box that is knee height or slightly lower.
Hold the stick in your right arm and put your right leg on the box.
Begin your squat by breaking at the knees. Get your knee close to the floor or all the way to the floor. Make sure to keep your chest tall.

name: Curtsy Lunge
steps: Step your left foot diagonally behind your right leg, crossing it behind your body. Bend your knees and lower your hips until your left thigh is parallel to the floor and your right knee is hovering just above the ground.
Push through your right foot to straighten your legs and return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with your other leg.

name: Forward Lunge
steps: Step forward with one leg and lower your body until your front thigh is parallel to the floor and your back knee almost touches the ground. Keep your chest up and your front foot flat on the floor.
Push through your front foot to straighten your legs and return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with your other leg.

name: Heels Up Squat
steps: Place your heels on a plate or very low step.
Keep your chest tall and initiate the squat by pushing your hips back and breaking at the knees.
You should notice yourself able to squat lower than normal.

name: Lateral Lunge
steps: Engage your core by drawing your belly button towards your spine. This will help to stabilize your body.
Take a large step to the side with your right foot, planting it firmly on the ground.
As you step, bend your right knee and lower your hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground. Your left leg should remain straight.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position. Repeat on the opposite side by stepping out with your left foot.

name: Bodyweight Reverse Lunge
steps: Take a big step backwards with your left leg, lowering your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground and your right knee is bent at a 90-degree angle.
Push through your right heel to return to standing, bringing your left leg back to starting position.
Repeat the movement on the other side, stepping backwards with your right leg and lowering your body until your right thigh is parallel to the ground and your left knee is bent at a 90-degree angle.

name: Split Squat
steps: Take a split stance. Make sure to keep your chest tall.
Bring your rear knee to the floor or close to it.
Drive through your front heel and your rear foot back to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Alternating Single Arm Press
steps: Start in a bench press position with two dumbbells.
Break at one of your elbows first while keeping the other elbow extended.
Your working arm should reach your side before pressing back to the ceiling.
Then alternate to the other arm.

name: Dumbbell Bayesian Lateral Raise
steps: Lay on an incline bench and let your arms freely.
Lateral raise your arms straight out to the side until your arms are about parallel with the ground.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
steps: Start by lying flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand.
Hold the dumbbells at chest level with your palms facing forward.
Engage your core and press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.

name: Dumbbell Chest Fly
steps: Lay on a bench with two dumbbells extended in front of you.
You should have a slight bend in your elbows and maintain that bend. Your shoulder joint should do all the movement.
Bring the dumbbells out to your side. Stop the dumbbells before going behind your body.
Bring the dumbbells back together at the top.

name: Dumbbell Decline Alternating Single Arm Press
steps: Lay on a decline bench with both arms extended in front of you.
Break at one of your arms first bringing that arm to your side while keeping the other arm extended.
Press the dumbbell back to the ceiling. Fully extend that arm before initiating on the other side.

name: Dumbbell Decline Bench Press
steps: Lay on a decline bench with both arms extended in front of you.
Break at the elbows and bring your upper arms to your side.
Once your upper arms have reached your side, press the dumbbells back to the ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Decline Chest Fly
steps: Lay flat on a decline bench press with the dumbbells pointed toward the ceiling.
Maintain a slight bend in your elbows. Bring the dumbbells out to the side, stopping the dumbbells before they pass your torso.
Bring the dumbbells together to finish the rep.

name: Dumbbell Decline Guillotine Bench Press
steps: Lie on the decline bench, grasp a pair of dumbbells and hold them at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to your chest, keeping your elbows flared out. As you lower the dumbbells, exhale deeply.
Once your elbows reach your chest, pause for a moment and then press them back up to the starting position

name: Dumbbell Decline Neutral Bench Press
steps: Lie on the decline bench, grasp a pair of dumbbells and hold them at shoulder level with your palms facing each other. Keep your elbows bent and close to your body.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to your chest, keeping your elbows bent and close to your body.
Once the dumbbells reach your chest, pause for a moment and then press them back up to the starting position.
This variation is much easier on the shoulder joints.

name: Dumbbell Decline Single Arm Bench Press
steps: Start by laying on a decline bench with two dumbbells.
Position the dumbbell above your chest with your elbow bent and shoulder level.
Slowly lower the dumbbell towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and your core engaged while the other dumbbell remains pointed toward the ceiling.
Once the dumbbell reaches your side, pause for a moment and then press the weight back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching to your other arm.

name: Dumbbell Decline Skullcrusher
steps: Start by lying on a decline bench with a dumbbell in each hand.
Hold the dumbbells above your chest with your arms extended and your palms facing each other. This is the starting position.
Slowly lower the dumbbells towards your forehead by bending your elbows. Keep your upper arms stationary and only move your forearms.
Once the dumbbells are close to your forehead, reverse the motion by extending your elbows and returning to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Decline Squeeze Press
steps: Lay flat on a decline bench with two dumbbells pressed to one another.
Break at the elbows and keep your elbows tucked as you bring the dumbbells to your chest.
Continue to press the dumbbells into one another throughout the rep.
Once the dumbbells reach your chest, press back toward the ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Forward Lunge
steps: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other.
Take a big step forward with your right foot, lowering your hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground and your left knee is hovering just above the floor.
Push through your right heel to straighten your right leg and return to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Bulgarian Split Squat
steps: Stand facing away from a bench or other elevated surface, holding a dumbbell in a goblet position.
Place the top of your left foot on the bench behind you, keeping your right foot planted on the ground.
Lower your hips down and back, bending your right knee and lowering your left knee until it almost touches the ground.
Push through your right heel to straighten your right leg and return to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Curtsy Lunge
steps: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and a dumbbell in a goblet position.
Step your left foot behind and to the right of your right foot, crossing it behind your right leg.
Bend your knees and lower your hips down and back, as if you were about to sit in a chair.
Push through your right heel to straighten your right leg and return to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Forward Lunge
steps: Hold a dumbbell in a goblet position.
Step forward with one of your legs while bending at your back leg. Bring your back knee to the floor or close to the floor.
Push through your front leg explosively to get back to your starting position.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Lateral Lunge
steps: Engage your core by drawing your belly button towards your spine. This will help to stabilize your body. Hold a dumbbell in a goblet position.
Take a large step to the side with your right foot, planting it firmly on the ground.
As you step, bend your right knee and lower your hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground. Your left leg should remain straight.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position. Repeat on the opposite side by stepping out with your left foot.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Reverse Lunge
steps: Hold a dumbbell in a goblet position.
Take a big step backwards with your left leg, lowering your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground and your right knee is bent at a 90-degree angle.
Push through your right heel to return to standing, bringing your left leg back to starting position.
Repeat the movement on the other side, stepping backwards with your right leg and lowering your body until your right thigh is parallel to the ground and your left knee is bent at a 90-degree angle.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Split Squat
steps: Hold a dumbbell in a goblet position.
Take a large step forward with one leg, and lower your body until your back knee nearly touches the ground. Your front knee should be bent at a 90-degree angle, and your back leg should be straight.
Push through the heel of your front foot to raise your body back to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Guillotine Bench Press
steps: Lie on the flat bench, grasp a pair of dumbbells and hold them at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to your chest, keeping your elbows flared out. As you lower the dumbbells, exhale deeply.
Once your elbows reach your chest, pause for a moment and then press them back up to the starting position

name: Dumbbell Guillotine Incline Bench Press
steps: Lie on the incline bench, grasp a pair of dumbbells and hold them at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to your chest, keeping your elbows flared out. As you lower the dumbbells, exhale deeply.
Once your elbows reach your chest, pause for a moment and then press them back up to the starting position

name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
steps: Lay flat on the incline bench with your feet on the ground. Raise the dumbbells until you have straight arms.
Lower the dumbbells to your mid chest
Raise the dumbbells until you've locked your elbows.

name: Dumbbell Incline Chest Fly
steps: Lay on an incline bench with two dumbbells extended in front of you.
You should have a slight bend in your elbows and maintain that bend. Your shoulder joint should do all the movement.
Bring the dumbbells out to your side. Stop the dumbbells before going behind your body.
Bring the dumbbells back together at the top.

name: Dumbbell Incline Hammer Curl
steps: Hold the dumbbells with a neutral grip (thumbs facing the ceiling). Seated on an incline bench.
Slowly lift the dumbbell up to chest height
Return to starting position and repeat.

name: Dumbbell Incline Reverse Curl
steps: Lay on an incline bench.
Grab the dumbbells with a pronated (overhand) grip. You can do this exercise thumbless if it's more comfortable on your wrists.
Flex at the elbows until your biceps touch your forearms. Try not to let your elbows flair outward.

name: Dumbbell Incline Zottman Curl
steps: Lay on an incline bench with your palms facing forward.
Flex at the elbows until your forearms touch your biceps.
When you hit the top of the movement, rotate your palms and then lower the dumbbells slowly.

name: Dumbbell Internally Rotated Chest Fly
steps: Lay on a bench with two dumbbells extended in front of you in an overhand grip.
You should have a slight bend in your elbows and maintain that bend. Your shoulder joint should do all the movement.
Bring the dumbbells out to your side. Stop the dumbbells before going behind your body.
Bring the dumbbells back together at the top.

name: Dumbbell Internally Rotated Incline Chest Fly
steps: Lay on an incline bench with two dumbbells extended in front of you.
You should have a slight bend in your elbows and maintain that bend. Your shoulder joint should do all the movement. You should have an overhand grip.
Bring the dumbbells out to your side. Stop the dumbbells before going behind your body.
Bring the dumbbells back together at the top.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Lunge Reach
steps: Engage your core by drawing your belly button towards your spine. This will help to stabilize your body. Hold a dumbbell in a goblet position.
Take a large step to the side with your right foot and reach toward the floor with your single dumbbell.
As you step, bend your right knee and lower your hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground. Your left leg should remain straight.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position. Repeat on the opposite side by stepping out with your left foot.

name: Dumbbell Laying 30 Degree Shrug
steps: Lay sideways on a bench letting your arm hang freely.
Pull your shoulder blade and joint toward the ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Laying Reverse Fly
steps: With dumbbells in either hand, lay on an incline bench that's at about 15-30 degrees.
With bent elbows, raise your arms up to shoulder level, pausing at the at the end of the motion.
Slowly lower your arms back to starting position.

name: Dumbbell Laying Reverse Fly Internally Rotated
steps: With dumbbells in either hand and an overhand grip, lay on an incline bench that is roughly 15 - 30 degrees high.
With bent elbows, raise your arms up to shoulder level, pausing at the at the end of the motion.
Slowly lower your arms back to starting position.

name: Dumbbell Laying Silverback Shrug
steps: Lay on an incline bench with your arms hanging freely.
Pull your shoulder blades back toward the ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Neutral Bench Press
steps: Lie on a flat bench, grasp a pair of dumbbells and hold them at shoulder level with your palms facing each other. Keep your elbows bent and close to your body.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to your chest, keeping your elbows bent and close to your body.
Once the dumbbells reach your chest, pause for a moment and then press them back up to the starting position.
This variation is much easier on the shoulder joints.

name: Dumbbell Neutral Incline Bench Press
steps: Lie on an incline bench, grasp a pair of dumbbells and hold them at shoulder level with your palms facing each other. Keep your elbows bent and close to your body.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to your chest, keeping your elbows bent and close to your body.
Once the dumbbells reach your chest, pause for a moment and then press them back up to the starting position.
This variation is much easier on the shoulder joints.

name: Dumbbell Neutral Seated Overhead Press
steps: Sit on an upright bench , grasp a pair of dumbbells and hold them at shoulder level with your palms facing each other. Keep your elbows bent and close to your body.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to your shoulder joint, keeping your elbows bent and close to your body.
Once the dumbbells reach your chest, pause for a moment and then press them back up to the starting position.
This variation is much easier on the shoulder joints.

name: Dumbbell Pullover
steps: Grab a dumbbell and hold with both hands. Point your dumbbell at the ceiling.
Flex at the shoulder joint while keeping your elbows mostly extended.
Flex until you feel a stretch in your lats then bring the dumbbell back to the ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
steps: Hold two dumbbells by your side.
Take a big step backwards with your left leg, lowering your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground and your right knee is bent at a 90-degree angle.
Push through your right heel to return to standing, bringing your left leg back to starting position.
Repeat the movement on the other side, stepping backwards with your right leg and lowering your body until your right thigh is parallel to the ground and your left knee is bent at a 90-degree angle.

name: Dumbbell Rolling Tricep Extension
steps: Lay flat on a bench with two dumbbells pointed toward the ceiling.
Break at the elbows until your forearms touch your biceps, then flex at the shoulder joint bringing your upper arms to the sides of your head.
Bring your elbows back up until they are pointed at the ceiling, then extend your elbows.

name: Dumbbell Seated Arnold Press
steps: Begin seated on an upright bench with your palms facing you.
Begin pressing toward the ceiling. As you press, rotate your palms.
As you extend your elbows fully your palms should be facing forward.

name: Dumbbell Squeeze Press
steps: Lay flat on a bench with two dumbbells pressed to one another.
Break at the elbows and keep your elbows tucked as you bring the dumbbells to your chest.
Continue to press the dumbbells into one another throughout the rep.
Once the dumbbells reach your chest, press back toward the ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Single Arm Press
steps: Start by laying on a bench with two dumbbells.
Position the dumbbell above your chest with your elbow bent and shoulder level.
Slowly lower the dumbbell towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and your core engaged while the other dumbbell remains pointed toward the ceiling.
Once the dumbbell reaches your side, pause for a moment and then press the weight back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before switching to your other arm.

name: Dumbbell Split Squat
steps: Hold a dumbbell in a goblet position.
Take a large step forward with one leg, and lower your body until your back knee nearly touches the ground. Your front knee should be bent at a 90-degree angle, and your back leg should be straight.
Push through the heel of your front foot to raise your body back to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Alternating Arnold Press
steps: Start in a standing position

name: Dumbbell Alternating Overhead Press
steps: Start with two dumbbells just above your shoulders.
Press one dumbbell to the ceiling while keeping the other in place.
Let the first dumbbell return to its starting position before pressing the other dumbbell.

name: Dumbbell Alternating Pendlay Row
steps: The dumbbells should be set on the ground. Hinge forward while keeping a flat back.
Pull one elbow back toward the ceiling and then return the dumbbell to the ground softly.
Then pull with the other arm.

name: Dumbbell Arnold Press
steps: Stand with both palms facing you.
Begin to press the dumbbells to the ceiling while rotating your palms.
When you reach elbow extension, your palms should be facing forward.

name: Dumbbell Figure Four Glute Bridge
steps: Lay flat on the ground with one leg crossed over the other and your heel planted on the ground.
Push your hips toward the ceiling and hold for a second.

name: Dumbbell Figure Four Hip Thrust
steps: Put your shoulder blades on a bench and cross one leg over the other.
Start with your hips up.
Drop your hips and then push your hips up toward the ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Front Rack Pause Squat
steps: Place two dumbbells on your shoulders and make sure you force your elbows upward.
Push your hips back and break at the knees.
When you reach parallel (hamstrings parallel with the ground) pause for 1-3 seconds. Then push through your heels back to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Front Rack Squat
steps: Place two dumbbells on your shoulders and push your elbows upward.
Push your hips back and break at the knees.
Hit parallel depth and then drive through your heels back to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Goblet Pulse Squat
steps: Hold a dumbbell in goblet position.
Squat down to parallel depth and then halfway back up.
Stay in this short range of motion for the duration of the set.

name: Dumbbell Half Kneeling Wood Chopper
steps: Take a half kneeling position. Start with the dumbbell by your knee on the side of the kneeling leg.
Rotate at the upper spine, holding the dumbbell away from your body with nearly extended elbows.

name: Dumbbell Heels Up Goblet Squat
steps: Take a half kneeling stance. The dumbbell should start over your knee that is up.
Rotate at the spine and point the dumbbell to the opposing side.

name: Dumbbell Heels Up Narrow Goblet Squat
steps: Stand with your feet on a plate. Your feet should be very close to one another, but not touching.
Begin your squat by pushing your hips back and breaking at the knees. Try to keep your chest tall while squatting.

name: Dumbbell Hollow Hold
steps: Lay flat on the ground with a dumbbell overhead.
Pull your upper back and legs off the ground. Make a banana shape with your body.
The dumbbell should be held in your hands and your ears should be between your biceps.

name: Dumbbell Kneeling Single Arm Row
steps: Place one hand and one knee on a bench. Make sure you keep a flat back.
Pull the dumbbell toward the ceiling.
Make sure to avoid any jerking or rotating.

name: Dumbbell Kneeling Wood Chopper
steps: Start in a kneeling position with your shoulder blades pulled back.
The dumbbell should begin by one of your hips.
Rotate at the spine and bring the dumbbell from your hip, 180 degrees around your body. The dumbbell should land at eye level.

name: Dumbbell Laying Incline Row
steps: Lay on an incline bench that is between 30-45 degrees high.
Let your arms hang freely. Pull your elbows straight back toward the ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Leg Curl
steps: Lay flat on a bench with a dumbbell between your feet.
Flex your knees and raise the dumbbell until the soles of your shoes are pointed at the ceiling.

name: Dumbbell Leg Extension
steps: Place a dumbbell between your feet. Lean back slightly and hold on to the bench.
Extend your knees until you feel your quads contract hard.

name: Dumbbell Neutral Alternating Overhead Press
steps: Start with two dumbbells near your shoulders with a neutral grip.
Press one of the dumbbells toward the ceiling while keeping the other in position.
Let the first dumbbell return to its starting position before pressing the other.

name: Dumbbell Neutral Overhead Press
steps: Start with both dumbbells near your shoulders in a neutral grip.
Press the dumbbells toward the ceiling.
Make sure you don't arch your back as you fatigue.

name: Dumbbell Offset Squat
steps: Hold a dumbbell over one shoulder.
Initiate the squat by pushing your hips back.
Avoid doing any twisting. Try to keep your shoulders even.

name: Dumbbell Overhead Press
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Bring the dumbbells to your shoulders, with your palms facing forward and your elbows bent.
From this starting position, extend your arms upwards so that the dumbbells are overhead.
Make sure to keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Overhead Side Bend
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell overhead.
From this starting position, bend to the side as far as you can while keeping your feet planted and your core engaged.

name: Dumbbell Overhead Tricep Extension
steps: Hold a dumbbell overhead.
Flex at the elbows until the dumbbell is behind your head.
Then extend your elbows until the dumbbell is back in the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Pendlay Row
steps: The dumbbells should be placed on the floor.
Hinge forward with a flat back and grab the dumbbells.
Pull both dumbbells back and your elbows toward the ceiling.
Return the dumbbells back to the floor softly on each rep.

name: Dumbbell Push Press
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in each hand.
From this starting position, dip your knees slightly and then quickly extend them as you press the dumbbells upwards.
Make sure to keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Renegade Row
steps: Start by getting into a plank position with a dumbbell in each hand. Your feet should be shoulder-width apart and your hands should be placed on the dumbbells, with your palms facing each other.
From this starting position, row one dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and your core engaged.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position, and repeat on the other side.
Make sure to keep your body straight and your hips level throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Seated Calf Raise
steps: Start by sitting on a bench with your feet shoulder-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand placed on each leg.
From this starting position, raise your heels as high as you can by pushing up through your toes.
Slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Extension
steps: Start by hinging over with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Bring the dumbbells to your sides, with your palms facing forward and your elbows slightly bent.
Make sure to keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Side Plank Up Down
steps: Start by lying on your side with your feet stacked on top of each other and a dumbbell on your hip.
Place your bottom elbow on the ground beneath your shoulder and engage your core to raise your hips off the ground.
Your hips will do all the moving. Think, "hips up, hips down."

name: Dumbbell Silverback Shrug
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder width apart holding dumbbells with both hands in front just past shoulder width.
Bend forward at the hips with a slight bend in your knees, keeping your back straight.
Engage your shoulder blades, as if you are trying to touch them together.

name: Dumbbell Single Arm Arnold Press
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Bring the dumbbell to your shoulder, with your palm facing you and your elbow bent.
From this starting position, extend your arm upwards so that the dumbbell is directly above your head.
As you extend your arm, rotate your palm to face away from your body.

name: Dumbbell Single Arm Neutral Overhead Press
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Bring the dumbbell to your shoulder, with your palm facing your body and your elbow bent.
From this starting position, press the dumbbell upwards so that it is directly above your shoulder.

name: Dumbbell Single Arm Overhead Press
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Bring the dumbbell to your shoulder, with your palm facing forward and your elbow bent.
From this starting position, extend your arm upwards so that the dumbbell is overhead.
Make sure to keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Single Arm Preacher Curl
steps: Start with a with a dumbbell in one hand and your elbow resting on the bench.
From this starting position, curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary on the bench.

name: Dumbbell Single Arm Row
steps: Start by standing in front of a flat bench with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Place your other hand on the bench for support, and bend at the waist so that your chest is parallel to the ground.
From this starting position, row the dumbbell up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blade together as you do so.
Make sure to keep your elbow close to your body and your core engaged throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Single Arm Row Knee
steps: Start by placing your elbow on one knee.
Hold a dumbbell in your other hand, with your palm facing inwards towards your body.
From this starting position, row the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and your core engaged.

name: Dumbbell Single Arm Spider Curl
steps: Lay on an incline or preacher curl bench.
Let your arms hang freely off the edge of the bench.
Fully flex your elbow and fully extend your elbow on each rep.

name: Dumbbell Single Leg Glute Bridge
steps: Start by lying on your back with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell on your hips.
Lift one leg off the ground and engage your glutes to raise your hips up towards the ceiling.
From this starting position, slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Single Leg Hip Thrust
steps: Start by lying on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell on your hips.
Lift one leg off the ground and engage your glutes to raise your hips up towards the ceiling.
From this starting position, slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Single Leg Single Arm Deadlift
steps: Start by standing on one leg with a dumbbell in one hand.
Slowly bend at the hips and lower the dumbbell towards the ground while extending your free leg behind you.
Keep your core engaged and your back straight as you lower the weight.
Once the dumbbell is close to the ground, push through your heel to return to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Single Leg Stiff Leg Deadlift
steps: Start by standing on one leg with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding two dumbbells.
From this starting position, hinge at the hips to lower the dumbbell towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Once you feel a stretch in your hamstring, slowly return to the starting position.
Make sure to keep your standing leg slightly bent and avoid rounding your back as you lower the dumbbell.

name: Dumbbell Spider Curl
steps: Start by placing your forearms on a flat bench and holding a dumbbell in each hand.
From this starting position, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body and your core engaged.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Staggered Deadlift
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Place one foot slightly behind the other, with the heel of your back foot elevated on a step or weight plate.
From this starting position, bend at the hips and knees to lower the dumbbells towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Once the dumbbells are below your knees, drive through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Staggered Glute Bridge
steps: Start by lying on your back with your feet on the ground and a dumbbell in each hand.
Place one foot on the ground and the other slightly in front, with your knees bent.
From this starting position, raise your hips up off the ground by pushing through your heels and squeezing your glutes.

name: Dumbbell Staggered Hip thrust
steps: Start by lying on your back on a bench with your feet on the ground and a dumbbell on your hips.
Place one foot on the ground and the other slightly in front, with your knees bent.
From this starting position, raise your hips up off the ground by pushing through your heels and squeezing your glutes.

name: Dumbbell Sumo Squat
steps: Start by standing with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Place the dumbbells on the ground between your feet.
From this starting position, bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat.
Make sure to keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Swing
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in both hands.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips to swing the dumbbell between your legs.
From this starting position, explosively extend your hips and swing the dumbbell up to chest height.
Allow the momentum of the swing to carry the dumbbell back down between your legs, and then repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Thruster
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Bring the dumbbells to your shoulders, with your palms facing inward and your elbows bent.
From this starting position, dip your knees slightly and then quickly extend them as you press the dumbbells upwards.
As you press the dumbbells upwards, also push your hips forward to help generate additional power.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Dumbbell Tricep Kickback
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Bend at the waist and place your opposite hand on your knee for support.
From this starting position, extend your arm backwards so that the dumbbell is behind your body.
Make sure to keep your elbow close to your body and your core engaged throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Twisting Curl
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Bring the dumbbells to your sides, with your palms facing inward and your elbows bent.
From this starting position, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders while rotating your wrists so that your palms face your shoulders at the top of the movement.

name: Dumbbell Hand Side Plank
steps: Start by lying on your side with your feet stacked on top of each other and a dumbbell on your hip.
Place your bottom hand on the ground beneath your shoulder and engage your core to raise your hips off the ground.
From this starting position, hold the side plank position for the desired amount of time, making sure to keep your body straight and your hips level.

name: Dumbbell Elbow Side Plank
steps: Take an elbow side plank position. Place a dumbbell on your hip.
Raise your hip up into position for the allotted amount of time.

name: Mountain Climber
steps: To perform a bodyweight mountain climber, start in a plank position with your hands placed directly under your shoulders.
Bring your right knee towards your chest, keeping your foot close to your hand. Quickly switch legs, bringing your left knee towards your chest.
Continue this movement, alternating legs, at a quick pace for a set amount of time or repetitions.

name: Burpee
steps: Start standing with your feet hip-width apart. Lower your body into a squat position, placing your hands on the ground in front of you.
Quickly jump your feet back into a plank position, keeping your body in a straight line from your head to your toes.
Perform a push-up, keeping your core tight and your elbows close to your body.
Jump your feet back towards your hands, and then jump up into the air, reaching your arms overhead.
Land softly and immediately lower your body back into the squat position to repeat the movement.

name: Plate Alternating Deadstop Row
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder width apart. Hold each plate to the side of you.
Bend forward at the hips bringing the plates to the floor while you slightly bend your knees, keeping your back straight.
One at a time, lift the plate up. Return the plate to the floor and do the other side.

name: Plate Bicep Curl
steps: Hold plate in both hands.
Flex your elbows until the plate nears your chest.
Extend your elbows until they are fully extended.

name: Plate Squat
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a weight plate in front of your chest with both hands.
Keep your back straight, lower your body as if you were sitting back into a chair.
Go as low as you can while keeping your heels flat on the ground, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.

name: Plate Clean And Press
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and the plate in front of the feet.
Bend the knees and hips to pick up the plate, keeping the back straight and core engaged.
Stand up, lock the hips and legs, and press the plate overhead with straight arms.
Lower the plate back to the starting position, keeping control of it, and repeat for desired reps.

name: Plate Squat Reach
steps: Stand holding a weight plate at chest height with both hands.
Lower into a squat, extending the plate in front of you at chest height
As you stand back up, pull the plate back into your chest.

name: Plate Clean
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and the plate in front of the feet.
Bend the knees and hips to pick up the plate, keeping the back straight and core engaged.
Stand up while pulling the plate towards the chest.
Keep shoulder level while keeping the elbows high and in front of the plate.

name: Plate Curl To Overhead Press
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and the plate in front of the torso, palms facing forward.
Curl the plate up to the front of the shoulder while keeping the elbows close to the body.
Rotate the palms to face forward and press the plate overhead, locking out the arms.
Lower the plate back to the starting position, keeping control of it, and repeat for desired reps.

name: Plate Squat Hold Reach
steps: Hold the plate with both hands at chest height.
Keeping your core tight and your chest up, squat down as low as you can go while keeping your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the squat position and reach the plate forward, keeping your arms straight and your back straight.
Return the plate to your chest while you hold the squat position and repeat reaching the plate in front of you

name: Plate Curl To Overhead Press To Tricep Extension
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and the plate in front of the torso, palms facing forward.
Curl the plate up to the front of the shoulder while keeping the elbows close to the body.
Rotate the palms to face forward and press the plate overhead, locking out the arms.
Lower the plate back behind the head to the starting position of a tricep extension.
Push the plate back up overhead, then lower it back to the starting position and repeat for desired reps.

name: Plate Curtsy Lunge
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and the plate in front of the torso.
Step back and across the body with the right foot, bending both legs to lower into a lunge position.
Push through the front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side by stepping back and across with the left foot.
Continue alternating sides for desired reps.

name: Plate Deadlift
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, the plate in front of the feet, and the knees slightly bent.
Bend the hips and knees to grip the plate with palms facing the body.
Keeping the back straight, stand up while lifting the plate off the ground.
Return the plate back to the starting position by bending the hips and knees, keeping control of the plate.

name: Plate Squat Hold Curl
steps: Lower into a squat position, keeping the plate at chest height.
Slowly curl the plate towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Hold for a moment at the top of the curl before lowering the plate back to chest height.
Repeat the curls as you hold the squat position.

name: Plate Single Leg Pinch Grip Deadlift
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and facing forwards. Grip the sides of a weight plate with your palms facing down.
Balance on one foot, as you raise the weight keep your arms and back straight, while you bring your  other foot back towards the ground
Keep four raised leg straight and in alignment with your back as you lower the plate back down to the starting position.

name: Plate Car Driver
steps: Inhale and brace your abdominals
Raise the arms vertically while keeping the elbows slightly bent
Once the arms are parallel with the floor, rotate the plate clockwise and counterclockwise.
Lower the plate back to the starting position.

name: Plate Front Raise
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and the plate in front of the torso, palms facing the thighs.
Keeping the elbows slightly bent, lift the plate up to the front of the body.
Raise the plate up until the arms are fully extended, maintaining control of the plate.
Lower the plate back to the starting position and repeat for desired reps.

name: Plate Good Morning Anterior
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, the plate held in front of you, and the knees slightly bent.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping the back straight, until the torso is parallel to the floor.
Push through the hips to return to the starting position.

name: Plate Good Morning
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, the plate held behind the neck and the knees slightly bent.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping the back straight, until the torso is parallel to the floor.
Push through the hips to return to the starting position.

name: Plate Halo
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and the plate held in front of your face with both hands, palms facing each other.
Rotate the plate around the head, keeping the arms extended and moving the plate in a circular motion.
Complete one full circle, then reverse the direction to complete another circle.

name: Plate Lateral Lunge
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and the plate held in front of the torso.
Take a step to the side with one foot, bending the knee and lowering the hips into a lunge.
Push through the front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side by stepping to the other side with the other foot.

name: Plate Forward Lunge
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and the plate held in front of the torso.
Take a step forward with one foot, bending the front knee to lower the hips into a lunge.
Push through the front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side by stepping forward with the other foot.

name: Band High Curl
steps: Anchor a band to a high point.
Start with your feet staggered and your elbows high.
Flex your elbows while keeping your arms high. Curl the band to your forehead.

name: Band High Hammer Curl
steps: Start with the band anchored to something high with your feet staggered and a neutral grip.
Keep your elbows high. Curl until your fists are at your forehead.

name: Band High Reverse Curl
steps: Start with a band attached to a high point, your feet staggered, and an overhand grip.
Flex your elbows pulling your forearms to your biceps. Your fists should land by your forehead.

name: Band Chest Fly
steps: Secure two resistance bands to a sturdy anchor point (e.g. a door anchor, a post, etc.) at chest height.
Stand facing away from the anchor point with feet shoulder-width apart and hold each end of the band in each hand.
Start with arms fully extended and parallel to the floor.
Keeping arms straight, slowly bring your arms together in front of your chest.

name: Band Chest Press
steps: Stand in the middle of the two bands with feet shoulder-width apart. Step forward with one foot to create tension in the bands.

Hold one band in each hand, palms facing forward.

Start the exercise with your arms extended in front of you, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower your arms to the sides of your chest, maintaining control of the band.
Push your arms back up to the starting position, keeping your core engaged and maintaining control of the band.

name: Band Curl
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, with the band under both feet.
Grasp the handles of the band with both hands, keeping your arms straight and extended in front of you.
Keep your back straight, abs engaged, and shoulders relaxed as you curl your hands towards your shoulders, bending at the elbows.

name: Plate Single Leg Deadlift
steps: Engage your core and straighten your legs to lift the plate off the ground.
Stand on one leg, keeping the other leg off the ground, while holding the plate in front of your body.
Keep your back straight and maintain a neutral head position while looking straight ahead.
Lower the plate back down to the ground while keeping your balance on one leg.

name: Band High Face Pull
steps: Start with a band anchored to a high point. Stagger your stance.
Pull the band to the bridge of your nose.

name: Plate Reverse Lunge Twist
steps: Step back with your right foot into a lunge position, both knees bent at 90 degrees.
Twist your torso to the right, keeping the plate close to your chest.
Push through your front foot to return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.

name: Plate Reverse Lunge
steps: Hold the plate at chest height close to your body
Take a big step backwards with your left leg, lowering your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground and your right knee is bent at a 90-degree angle.
Push through your right heel to return to standing, bringing your left leg back to starting position.
Repeat the movement on the other side, stepping backwards with your right leg and lowering your body until your right thigh is parallel to the ground and your left knee is bent at a 90-degree angle.

name: Band Hip Abduction
steps: Attach the band around your ankle. Stand with feet hip-width apart, feet facing forward, and hands on hips.
Lift your right leg away from your body to the side, keeping your knee straight. This is your starting position.

Maintaining proper form, move your right leg as far to the side as you can, keeping your left leg planted firmly on the ground. Hold for a second and then return your right leg to the starting position.

name: Band Hip Adduction
steps: Attach a band to a stable anchor point, such as a door handle or post.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and place the band around your ankles.
Keeping your feet together, move your legs out to the sides and then back to the midline of your body. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Band Internally Rotated Chest Fly
steps: Start with both feet hip-width apart and step on the middle of the band with both feet. Hold the handles of the band in each hand and position the band above your chest, with your palms facing down.

Keeping your elbows slightly bent, slowly raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel with the floor. Make sure to internally rotate your shoulder joints by bringing your shoulder blades together and rotating your forearms inward towards your torso.
Hold the position for a few seconds and then slowly lower your arms back to the starting position, keeping the internal rotation of your shoulder joints throughout the movement.

name: Plate Pinch Grip Side Bend
steps: Stand straight with the plate held at your side
Keep your hips and shoulders facing forward, bend to one side slowly
Return to the upright position.
Switch hands and repeat

name: Band Leg Curl
steps: Loop the band around a sturdy anchor point such as a weightlifting rack or a door handle.
Lie on your back with your heels placed inside the band. Make sure the band is centered under your heels and your feet are hip-width apart.
Using your hamstrings, curl your heels towards your glutes while keeping your hips on the ground.

name: Plate Pinch Grip Row
steps: Make sure to keep your elbow close to your body and your core engaged throughout the movement.
Bend forward at your hips while maintaining a flat back.
Pull the plate toward your upper abdomen.
Lower the weight in a controlled manner and repeat.

name: Band Leg Extension
steps: Anchor a band to something stable and set up a bench.
Run through the band underneath the bench and loop around your feet.
Flex and extend your knees.

name: Band Low Lateral Raise
steps: Anchor a band to a low point and stand with the band crossing your body.
While keeping your elbow mostly extended, lift your arm until it's parallel with the floor.

name: Band Mid Lateral Raise
steps: Anchor a band to a point that is the same height as your fist when your arms are at your side and stand with the band crossing your body.
While keeping your elbow mostly extended, lift your arm until it's parallel with the floor.

name: Plate Pinch Grip Deadlift
steps: Stand facing the plate with your feet shoulder-width apart and the plate positioned between your feet.
Bend down and grasp the plate from the top edges with both hands, making sure your thumbs and fingers are evenly spaced.
Keep your back straight, chest up and core engaged, and stand up with the plate, keeping it close to your body.
Lower the plate back down to the ground, keeping control and maintaining good posture.

name: Band Overhead Press
steps: Stand between the two anchored bands, grasp them with an overhand grip, and position the bands at shoulder level.
Brace your core and push the bands overhead, fully extending your arms.
Lower the bands back to shoulder level, keeping your core engaged and arms straight. Repeat for desired number of reps.

name: Plate Overhead Tricep Extension
steps: Stand or sit with a weight plate in your hands, arms extended straight above your head
Lower the weight plate behind your head by bending at the elbows.
Keep your elbows close to your head and shoulders relaxed.
Push the weight plate back up to starting position, extending your arms fully.

name: Band Rapunzel Pushdown
steps: Anchor the band to a high point and face away. Hold the band with both hands, placing your hands shoulder-width apart and keeping your elbows close to your body.
Slowly extend your arms downward, keeping your elbows close to your body. Push the band down as far as you can, feeling the contraction in your triceps.

name: Plate Overhead Squat
steps: Hold a weight plate at arm's length overhead, with your arms fully extended. The plate should be directly above your head and your elbows should be locked.
Begin to bend your knees, lowering your body as far as you can while keeping the weight plate overhead. Make sure to keep your back straight and your chest lifted.
Once your thighs are parallel to the ground or as low as you can go, pause for a moment.
Push through your heels to return to a standing position, keeping the weight plate overhead the entire time.

name: Band Rear Delt Fly
steps: Start by standing between the two anchored bands with your feet shoulder-width apart and the bands positioned at a high point.
Grasp the ends of each band with your hands, keeping your palms facing each other.

Keeping your arms slightly bent and your back straight, slowly pull the bands away from your body until your arms are extended straight out to the sides. Hold for a second, then slowly release back to the starting position. Repeat for desired reps.

name: Band Reverse Curl
steps: Start by standing on the center of the band with both feet, shoulder-width apart.
Hold the band with both hands, palms facing down, arms extended straight in front of you.
Slowly curl your hands towards your shoulders, pulling the band tight, then lower your arms back to the starting position, keeping tension on the band throughout the exercise.

name: Plate Overhead Press To Tricep Extension
steps: Brace your core and press the weight plate overhead until your arms are fully extended. Keep your shoulders down and back.
While your arms are fully extended, lower the weight behind your head to perform the extension
After completing the extension, lower the weight to your chest, and perform the overhead press. Repeat

name: Band Single Arm Pushdown
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band with your right hand, with your arm at your side.
Flex and extend your elbow.

name: Single Arm Rapunzel Pushdown
steps: Stand with a band anchored to a high point. Face away from the band. Hold the loop in one hand.
Keep your elbow tucked in to your side. Extend your elbow. When you extend, don't let your upper arm raise. The band should land over your shoulder.

name: Band Single Arm Skullcrusher
steps: Stand facing away from a band which is anchored to a high point.
Keep your elbow pointed forward, and extend and flex your elbow.

name: Plate Deficit Lunge
steps: Stand on a plate with both feet.
Step back with one foot to the floor and let your back knee get close to or tap the floor.
Push through your front leg until both feet are back on the plate.

name: Plate Deficit Pushup
steps: Start in a pushup position with both hands on a plate. This will extend your range of motion.
Break at the elbows and shoulders until your upper arm travels behind the body slightly.
Then press back into the original position.

name: Plate Full Lateral Raise
steps: Stand tall holding two plates at your side.
Lateral raise the plates to your side and continue until the plates are pointed at the ceiling.
Let your shoulder joint naturally rotate. At the bottom you should have a neutral or overhand grip. By the time the plates are overhead, your palms should be facing forward.

name: Plate Glute Bridge to Chest Press
steps: Lay flat on the ground with a plate on your chest.
Push your hips up toward the ceiling and maintain that extended position.
With your hips up, press the plate toward the ceiling.

name: Plate Glute Bridge to Pullover
steps: Lay flat on the ground with a plate on your chest.
Push your hips up toward the ceiling and maintain that extended position.
Extend your elbows mostly, break at the shoulder joint while maintaining hip extension and the slight bend in your elbows.
Let the plate tap the floor (or get close) and then pull the plate back into the starting position.

name: Plate Internally Rotated Rear Delt Fly
steps: With plates in either hand, bend your knees with your feet slightly bowed out. Hinge forward with a flat back.
Maintain an overhand grip and internally rotated shoulders.
Raise your arms backward toward the ceiling while maintaining a light band in your elbows.

name: Plate Lateral Raise
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip two plates in each hand at your sides.
Keeping your arms straight, raise the plates out to your sides until they are in line with your shoulders.
Lower the plates back to your sides with control. Repeat for desired number of reps.

name: Plate Overhead Carry
steps: Start by standing upright with your feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a single weight plate overhead with both hands, keeping your arms extended straight above your head.
Begin walking forward, taking slow and steady steps while maintaining a straight line. Your focus should be on maintaining balance and control of the weight plate overhead.

As you walk, continue to keep your arms extended and the weight plate overhead. Try to maintain good posture and avoid leaning forward or backward, as this could result in losing control of the weight plate.
Continue walking for a set distance or for a certain amount of time, keeping your focus on proper form and technique. When finished, return to the starting position and repeat as desired.

name: Plate Overhead Shrug
steps: Stand tall with a weight plate overhead.
Elevated and depress your scapula.
Avoid leaning forward or backwards to keep pressure off of your lumbar spine.

name: Plate Overhead Walking Lunge
steps: Stand tall with a plate overhead.
Step with one foot forward bringing your back knee to the floor. Gently tap your knee then drive through your front leg.
Go back to a standing position and then step forward with the opposing legs.

name: Plate Pinch Grip Farmer's Carry
steps: Stand tall holding two plates in each hand with a pinch grip.
Walk for the desired distance or time.

name: Plate Rear Delt Fly
steps: Keep your back straight and bend forward slightly at the waist.
With arms extended, raise the weights out to your sides until they are in line with your shoulders.
Pause briefly and then lower the weights back to the starting position.

name: Plate Russian Twist
steps: Start by sitting on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground
Engage your core muscles by pulling your belly button toward your spine.
Slowly twist your upper body to the right, keeping your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold for a second and then twist to the left, holding for another second.

name: Plate Situp
steps: Start by lying on your back on a mat with your legs bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a weight plate over your head with both hands, making sure to keep your arms straight.
Engage your core and use your abdominal muscles to sit up, keeping the weight plate towards the ceiling.

name: Plate Squat Hold
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, with a weight plate held in both hands at chest height.
Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight and your weight in your heels.
Go down until your thighs are parallel to the floor, then pause for a moment.
Hold the squat position for the desired amount of time, keeping the weight plate steady and your core engaged.

name: Weighted Forearm Plank
steps: Get into a kneeling position with both forearms pressed into the ground.
Place a weight plate on your back (or have a partner do it for you).
Hold the plank for the allotted amount of time.

name: Plate Staggered Deadlift
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding a weight plate.
Place one foot slightly behind the other, with the heel of your back foot elevated.
From this starting position, bend at the hips and knees to lower the plate towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Once the plate is below your knees, drive through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.

name: Plate Staggered Pinch Grip Deadlift
steps: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding two plates with a pinch grip.
Place one foot slightly behind the other, with the heel of your back foot elevated.
From this starting position, bend at the hips and knees to lower the plates towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.

name: Plate Thruster
steps: Start with the plate at chest height. Begin a squat.
Make sure to hit parallel depth. On your way back up, use the momentum you generate with your legs to help push the plate into an overhead position.
Let the plate return to chest height before initiating the next squat.

name: Plate Reach
steps: Start in a standing position with a plate against your chest.
Press the plate away from your body until your elbows are extended.

name: Plate Standing Twist
steps: Stand with a plate extended away from your body. Elbows mostly extended.
Rotate at the upper spine while keeping your lumbar spine in a neutral position.
Do not let the plate go behind your body.

name: Weighted Hand Plank
steps: Start in a kneeling position with your hands planted on the ground.
Place a weight plate on your back or have a friend do it for you.
Pick your knees up off the ground. Hold in this position with a flat back.

name: Plate Hollow Hold
steps: Lay flat on the ground with your arms extended over head holding a weight plate.
Pick your upper back and your legs off the ground. Your body should be in a banana shape.

name: Plate Wood Chopper
steps: Stand with a shoulder width stance. Begin with the plate near one of your quads.
Rotate at your upper spine to engage the obliques.
You can pivot your back foot to keep from generating too much torque at the knee.

name: Barbell Feet Elevated Figure Four Glute Bridge
steps: Start by lying flat on your back with your feet resting on top of a box, the barbell placed over your hips, and cross one leg over the other.
Push through your heels and raise your hips towards the ceiling, keeping your feet flat on the box.
Engage your glutes at the top of the movement, pause briefly and then lower your hips back down to the starting position.

name: Barbell Feet Elevated Glute Bridge
steps: Start by lying flat on your back with your feet resting on top of a box, the barbell placed over your hips.
Push through your heels and raise your hips towards the ceiling, keeping your feet flat on the box.
Engage your glutes at the top of the movement, pause briefly and then lower your hips back down to the starting position.
